title: rubus foliosus subsp. pinicola
intro: Rubus foliosus subsp. pinicola, also known as the Mountain Dewberry, is a subspecies of the dewberry plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds and reduce inflammation. The Mountain Dewberry is believed to have various health benefits, including its potential to help lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its potential health benefits.
